insight_source_id.title: Source ID
insight_source_id.description: Stable identifier for this Copilot connection (e.g., 'github-copilot-acme'). Required non-empty — the composite unique_key for org_metrics depends on it to discriminate between multiple Copilot connections under the same tenant.

github_token.title: GitHub Personal Access Token
github_token.description: Classic Personal Access Token with 'manage_billing:copilot' scope (required for the seats endpoint). Either 'manage_billing:copilot' or 'read:org' is sufficient for the metrics reports endpoints. Must be created by an Organization Owner. Fine-grained PATs do not support these scopes. The 'Copilot usage metrics' policy must be enabled under Organization Settings → Copilot → Policies, otherwise metrics endpoints return HTTP 403.

github_org.title: GitHub Organization Slug
github_org.description: GitHub organization slug (e.g., 'my-company') with active Copilot for Business or Enterprise subscription.

github_start_date.title: Start Date
github_start_date.description: Earliest date for metrics backfill (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to 90 days ago if omitted. Note: Copilot reports API only has data from 2025-10-10 onwards; earlier dates return HTTP 204 (no content).

metrics_lookback_days.title: Metrics Re-fetch Lookback (days)
metrics_lookback_days.description: On each incremental run, the org/user daily-metrics streams re-query this many days before the saved cursor. Copilot daily reports lag (often >24-48h; weekends later) and can be restated, so without a lookback a recent day that returned HTTP 204 (report not ready yet) would be permanently skipped once the cursor advanced past it. Re-fetched days are deduped downstream by unique_key (ReplacingMergeTree), so the overlap is idempotent. Default 7.
